1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter) for informal mathematical reasoning. Useful for a second opinion or a paper-style sketch.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Dispatch synchronously** (not via background `Bash` with `run_in_background: true`). Background dispatch leaves the parent's session log stuck on "running in background" on the dashboard.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | — | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | — (review owns) | — | **write** (reset at review start) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and clears the file after the plan phase succeeds. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	## Agent Roles
112	
113	### Plan Agent
114	
115	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
116	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
117	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
118	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
119	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
120	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
121	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
122	
123	### Prover Agent
124	
125	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
126	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/` (`prover-autoformalize.md` / `prover-prover.md` / `prover-polish.md`).
127	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
128	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
129	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
130	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
131	
132	### Subagents
133	
134	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
135	
136	### Review Agent
137	
138	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
139	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
140	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
141	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
142	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
143	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
144	
145	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
146	
147	These run automatically between phases:
148	
149	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
150	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
151	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.
152

1	# Prover — Prover Stage
2	
3	You fill `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs in your assigned `.lean` file.
4	
5	## Workflow
6	
7	1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
8	2. Read `task_pending.md` for prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas on your file.
9	3. Check the `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments (file-specific user hints).
10	4. **Read the relevant blueprint chapter before writing Lean code.** The chapter holds the mathematical proof sketch you must align with. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
11	5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs. Push as far as possible.
12	6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you can't fully close a sorry, leave your best attempt — commented-out steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` if needed), but your work must be visible for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to a bare `sorry` — that wastes the work.
13	7. Write your results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
14	
15	**Write permissions**: only your assigned `.lean` file(s) and `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, blueprint chapters, or other agents' files.
16	
17	## Protected declarations
18	
19	`archon-protected.yaml` lists declarations with **frozen signatures**. You may fill their proof bodies but must not rename, re-type, reorder arguments, or weaken hypotheses. Only the mathematician edits protected signatures.
20	
21	## Avoid early termination
22	
23	- Don't abandon a proof prematurely. Many complex proofs run to thousands of lines.
24	- Difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave a `sorry`.
25	- Don't delegate to "the next iteration" or "another prover" if more effort could close it.
26	- Only modify the proof for your assigned task — leave unrelated proofs untouched.
27	- **Decompose**: break the proof into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure when available: L1, L2, L3, …) and solve each individually until the entire goal closes.
28	
29	## Task completion criteria
30	
31	Your task is complete ONLY when ALL of:
32	
33	1. Every `sorry` in scope is replaced with a complete proof.
34	2. Zero axioms introduced.
35	3. The file compiles cleanly.
36	
37	## Never weaken the type to dodge the proof
38	
39	When the substantive type is unattainable this iter, you have one honest move: leave `sorry` with the **intended type signature** so the type itself encodes the claim. You must NOT substitute a tautologically-true type to trivialize the body. Three patterns are banned:
40	
41	- **Reflexive-iso placeholder.** Replacing a non-trivial `X ≅ Y` (or `Nonempty (X ≅ Y)`) target with `Nonempty (X ≅ X) := ⟨Iso.refl _⟩`. The type now asserts nothing; the docstring's "iter-N placeholder" disclosure does not undo the structural lie, and the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference will resolve to this hollow declaration.
42	- **`Classical.choice` body around an explicit witness.** Writing `Classical.choice (α := X) ⟨witness⟩` to dissolve `Type` vs `Prop` friction — the witness is unreachable through any unfold, so the def is indistinguishable from `Classical.choice ⟨any-other-X⟩` downstream. Either use `Classical.choose` on the existential chain (preserves the spec), or `Nonempty.some`-with-witness; or ship a typed `sorry`.
43	- **Empty-content `proof_wanted`.** `proof_wanted` discards the declaration post-elaboration — the named decl never enters the environment, so the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference breaks. Use `theorem name : T := by sorry` with the intended signature instead.
44	
45	**Litmus test**: if you `unfold` your declaration, does it expose the named substantive content (Kähler differential module, the explicit iso, …) or does it stop at `Classical.choice` / `Iso.refl _` / nothing? If the latter, the body is structurally vacuous — ship the typed `sorry` instead.
46	
47	## When infrastructure is missing
48	
49	Do NOT report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up:
50	
51	1. **Use the informal agent** (`.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py`): "Prove [goal] without using [missing infrastructure], only Mathlib." Even an imperfect sketch is valuable.
52	2. **Try the alternative** — formalize whatever the informal agent suggests.
53	3. **If you still can't**: write the alternative sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md` and record in your task result what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found. "I couldn't prove X, but here's approach Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than "infrastructure missing".
54	
55	When stuck more generally: break into smaller subgoals, search Mathlib more thoroughly, prove missing helpers yourself, try alternative strategies, re-read the blueprint, use Web Search for published proofs.
56	
57	**Impossibility vs difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.
58	
59	## Proof style
60	
61	- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its body.
62	- Keep edits minimal; don't delete comments or change labels; don't add unrelated declarations.
63	- Helper lemmas you introduced may be modified if they turn out wrong.
64	- Add a concise comment above each helper lemma so reuse is easy.
65	- **`change` vs `show`** — `change` reshapes the goal up to defeq; `show` is purely display-level annotation. Using `show` where `change` is needed produces a linter warning. Default to `change` when in doubt.
66	
67	## Mathlib tags in PROGRESS.md
68	
69	The plan agent tags suggested lemmas:
70	
71	- `[verified]` — confirmed to exist.
72	- `[expected]` — guessed by naming convention. Quick `lean_local_search`; pivot if it doesn't exist.
73	- `[gap]` — verified NOT in Mathlib. Don't waste search time; formalize a workaround.
74	
75	## LSP MCP tools
76	
77	The `archon-lean-lsp` server exposes Lean LSP operations as **MCP tool calls** (full names like `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_goal` / `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages`). The lean4 skill reference uses short names (`lean_goal`, `lean_local_search`, …) — same tools, NOT shell binaries.
78	
79	- Always invoke through your tool-call interface, the same way as `Read` or `Grep`.
80	- **Never** call `Bash` with `lean_goal …` etc. — there is no such shell command.
81	- First LSP action: `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages` on your file. If it returns `success: false` the server is cold; retry once or run `lake build` once via Bash, then retry.
82	
83	## Search protocol
84	
85	Follow `references/lean-lsp-tools-api.md`. Key priority:
86	
87	1. `lean_local_search` first.
88	2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — describe the mathematical content, not just the name.
89	3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only.
90	4. Never use shell `find` / `grep` to locate Mathlib theorems.
91	
92	## Tooling traps
93	
94	- **Trust `goals_after`, not just empty diagnostics**: in `lean_multi_attempt`, `diagnostics: []` can be misleading (line-scoping quirks attach errors to the enclosing `by` block). Check that `goals_after` is empty or has advanced.
95	- **Beware `lean_run_code` with imports**: standalone snippets can silently swallow elaboration errors when they contain imports. Rely on the actual file's `lean_diagnostic_messages` for authoritative verification.
96	
97	## Logging
98	
99	Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` (mirror your `.lean` path: `Algebra/WLocal.lean` → `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`):
100	
101	```markdown
102	# Algebra/WLocal.lean
103	
104	## wLocal_iff (line 45)
105	### Attempt 1
106	- **Approach:** Direct case split on maximal ideals
107	- **Result:** FAILED — needed IsLocalRing instance, not available
108	- **Dead end:** direct case split without IsLocalRing
109	
110	### Attempt 2
111	- **Approach:** Stacks 0A31, characterize via bijection on spectra
112	- **Result:** RESOLVED
113	- **Key insight:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective` bridges the gap
114	
115	## helper_bijective (line 78)
116	### Attempt 1
117	- **Approach:** Split into injectivity + surjectivity; prove injectivity
118	- **Result:** PARTIAL (branch closed) — injectivity closed; surjectivity remains
119	- **Next step:** `PrimeSpectrum.range_comap_of_surjective` for the right branch
120	- **Lemmas found:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_surjective`
121	```
122	
123	**Rules**: one section per theorem/lemma in your file. Each attempt records approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / PARTIAL / IN PROGRESS), dead-end warnings or next steps. Log negative search results ("Searched 'projective module infinite rank' — nothing in Mathlib"). The plan agent merges these into `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md` next iter.
124	
125	**Read-only context**: read `task_pending.md` (prior attempts on your file) and `task_done.md` (when the problem resembles a completed one). Do not write to either.
126	
127	## End-of-session handoff
128	
129	Before stopping:
130	
131	1. Write `task_results/<your_file>.md` with: current result, lemmas discovered, concrete next step, dead-end warnings.
132	2. Save all changes; ensure the file compiles (use scoped `sorry` for stuck parts if needed).
133

92	/-- Algebra-level core: for `k` a field and `B` a commutative `k`-algebra of
93	finite type, if `b : B` satisfies `D b = 0` in `Ω_{B/k}` then `b ∈
94	range (algebraMap k B)`. This is the chart-algebra (β-core ring-side) helper
95	of the iter-144 piece (ii) pivot, formalising the "constants = kernel of
96	universal derivation" content of the Kähler derivation.

101	pending the iter-148+ closure of:

103	  (`Mathlib.RingTheory.Derivation.DifferentialRing`); the typeclass is
104	  positioned for `Differential B` (a specific derivation `B → B`) rather
105	  than `KaehlerDifferential.D : B → Ω_{B/k}`, so the bridge requires
106	  packaging the universal derivation through the typeclass.

108	  = B^p` (Cartier direction; Stacks Tag 07F4) — this is a Mathlib gap-
109	  fill that the project decomposes into substeps (p1.a)–(p1.d) per the

116	type `k`-algebras in characteristic `p > 0` (e.g. `B = k[x]`, `b = x^p`
117	has `D b = 0` but `b ∉ range (algebraMap k B)` unless `x ∈ k`). The
118	iter-146 disposition (B.preferred) concentrates the chart-of-proper-
119	curve descent at the helper, which the consumer
120	`GrpObj.df_zero_factors_through_constant_on_chart` below invokes in tandem.
121	The lemma as stated remains the blueprint-mandated entry point per

123	theorem KaehlerDifferential.mem_range_algebraMap_of_D_eq_zero
124	    {k : Type u} [Field k]
125	    {B : Type u} [CommRing B] [Algebra k B] [Algebra.FiniteType k B]
126	    {b : B} (hDb : _root_.KaehlerDifferential.D k B b = 0) :
127	    b ∈ (algebraMap k B).range := by
128	  -- Step 1 (kernel-of-D ⊇ range algebraMap): this direction is the
129	  -- standard `Derivation.map_algebraMap` and is the reverse of the
130	  -- statement; recorded here as the reverse inclusion the consumer may
131	  -- combine with the forward direction below.
132	  have _hRev : ∀ a : k, _root_.KaehlerDifferential.D k B (algebraMap k B a) = 0 := by
133	    intro a
134	    -- `KaehlerDifferential.D` is a `Derivation k B Ω[B⁄k]`; the
135	    -- algebraMap-image vanishes by `Derivation.map_algebraMap`.
136	    exact (_root_.KaehlerDifferential.D k B).map_algebraMap a
137	  -- Step 2 (forward direction, the substantive content): the iter-148
138	  -- prover lane attempted path (p2) char-0 via `Differential.ContainConstants`

145	  --   (BR.2) Basis selection `{dx_1, .., dx_n}` of `Ω_{B/k}` via

149	  --          basis (no off-the-shelf Mathlib lemma; ~30–50 LOC).
150	  --   (BR.4) `Differential B` instance per `∂_i` (`Derivation ℤ B B`
151	  --          structure; the `∂_i` is `k`-linear hence `ℤ`-linear).
152	  --   (BR.5) `Differential.ContainConstants k B` instance for the
153	  --          chosen `∂_i` in `CharZero k` — Mathlib has the class
154	  --          definition but no instance for the standard-smooth case.

156	  -- Sub-gaps (BR.3)–(BR.5) form an ~80–150 LOC Mathlib-PR-grade bridge

175	structure, a chart-section `b ∈ B` with vanishing universal Kähler derivation
176	`KaehlerDifferential.D k B b = 0` lies in `range (algebraMap k B)` — the chart-
177	local kernel-of-`D` content of the iter-144 chart-algebra pivot piece (ii) is
178	the algebra-level statement that constants are the image of the base field.

182	irreducible over `Spec k`); the chart-ring `B` is carried as a separate finite-
183	type `k`-algebra argument with the (chart-side identification `B ≃ₐ[k] Γ(V, O_C)`
184	deferred to the consumer site via the `Scheme.Over.ext_of_diff_zero` refinement

193	(3) 2-chart Čech Mayer–Vietoris on `Ω_{C/k}^{⊕n}` reusing
194	`Cohomology/MayerVietoris*`; (4) 2-chart-cover existence per Stacks 0F8L;

214	/-- Integrally-closed-constants helper: in a (smooth) proper geometrically
215	irreducible scheme `X` over a base field `k`, the global sections
216	`Γ(X, O_X)` are exhausted by the image of the structure-morphism's
217	action on the top open, i.e.\ the `appTop` ring hom of the structure
218	morphism `X ⟶ Spec k` is surjective. Equivalently, `Γ(X, O_X) ≅ k` as
219	`k`-algebras (modulo the canonical iso `Γ(Spec k, ⊤) ≃ k`).
220	
221	Blueprint: `chapters/RigidityKbar.tex` §
222	`\lem:constants_integral_over_base_field`. Three-substep recipe (per
223	the iter-146 prover-lane reading):
224	(1) `IsReduced X` + `GeometricallyIrreducible (X ↘ Spec k)` (over the
225	    singleton-base `Spec k`) ⇒ `IsIntegral X` (combine
226	    `GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace_of_subsingleton` with
227	    `isIntegral_of_irreducibleSpace_of_isReduced`).
228	(2) `IsIntegral X` + `UniversallyClosed (X ↘ Spec k)` (from
229	    `IsProper`) ⇒ `Γ(X, O_X)` is a field
230	    (`isField_of_universallyClosed`); and additionally
231	    `LocallyOfFiniteType (X ↘ Spec k)` ⇒ the `appTop` map is finite
232	    (`finite_appTop_of_universallyClosed`). So `Γ(X, O_X)` is a finite
233	    field extension of `k`.
234	(3) Geometric irreducibility ⇒ this field extension is trivial via
235	    base change to `\bar k`: `Γ(X, O_X) ⊗_k \bar k ≃ Γ(X_{\bar k},
236	    ⊤) = \bar k` (the latter equality by the same chain applied to
237	    `X_{\bar k}` over `\bar k`, which is integral after base change
238	    because geometric irreducibility is stable under base change).
239	    A finite field extension `Γ` of `k` with `Γ ⊗_k \bar k = \bar k`
240	    has `dim_k Γ = 1`, hence `Γ = k`.
241	
242	Iter-146 prover lane: signature refined to its real shape; substeps
243	(1)–(2) of the chain are closed by chasing the Mathlib lemmas
244	identified by the planner; substep (3) (the geom-irr base-change step)
245	remains a structured `sorry`. The `[IsReduced X]` hypothesis is added
246	explicitly because Mathlib snapshot `b80f227` lacks the lemma
247	`Smooth ⇒ IsReduced` over a field — see the `Rigidity.lean` "Hypothesis
248	history" block for the same explicit-`IsReduced` discipline. -/
249	theorem constants_integral_over_base_field
250	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {X : Scheme.{u}} [X.Over (Spec (.of k))]
251	    [IsProper (X ↘ Spec (.of k))]
252	    [Smooth (X ↘ Spec (.of k))]
253	    [IsReduced X]
254	    [GeometricallyIrreducible (X ↘ Spec (.of k))] :
255	    RingHom.range ((X ↘ Spec (CommRingCat.of k)).appTop.hom) = ⊤ := by
256	  -- Substep (1): IsReduced + GeometricallyIrreducible (over Spec k,
257	  -- a singleton) ⇒ X integral.
258	  haveI : IrreducibleSpace X :=
259	    GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace_of_subsingleton
260	      (X ↘ Spec (CommRingCat.of k))
261	  haveI : IsIntegral X := isIntegral_of_irreducibleSpace_of_isReduced X
262	  -- Substep (2.a): IsProper extends UniversallyClosed, so Γ(X, ⊤) is a
263	  -- field by `isField_of_universallyClosed`.
264	  have _hΓfield : IsField ↑(X.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ⊤)) :=
265	    isField_of_universallyClosed k (X ↘ Spec (CommRingCat.of k))
266	  -- Substep (2.b): the `appTop.hom` ring map is finite by
267	  -- `finite_appTop_of_universallyClosed` (uses `LocallyOfFiniteType`,
268	  -- supplied by `IsProper`).
269	  have _hAppTopFinite :
270	      ((X ↘ Spec (CommRingCat.of k)).appTop.hom).Finite :=
271	    finite_appTop_of_universallyClosed k (X ↘ Spec (CommRingCat.of k))
272	  -- Substep (3) (iter-148 smart-proof path (b)): reduce the
273	  -- surjectivity goal to the conjunction
274	  --   `IsPurelyInseparable k Γ(X, ⊤) ∧ Algebra.IsSeparable k Γ(X, ⊤)`
275	  -- and discharge via Mathlib's
276	  -- `IsPurelyInseparable.surjective_algebraMap_of_isSeparable`.
277	  --
278	  -- The iter-147 path (a) BUILD-IT chain (algclose base change + step
279	  -- (e) flat-base-change-of-Γ-for-proper-schemes) is moved below the
280	  -- main scaffold as a backup; the iter-148 prover lane commits to
281	  -- path (b) per `PROGRESS.md` § "Iter-148 planner path commitment".
282	  --
283	  -- Path (b) summary:
284	  --   (b.1) `Smooth (X ↘ Spec k)` ⇒ `Algebra.IsSeparable k Γ(X, ⊤)`.
285	  --         Mathlib b80f227 gap: no `Smooth ⇒ IsGeometricallyReduced`
286	  --         instance, and no `geom-reduced finite ext ⇒ separable`
287	  --         bridge in the snapshot. Two named structural sub-gaps:
288	  --         (S3.sep.1) `Smooth ⇒ Algebra.IsGeometricallyReduced k Γ`
289	  --         and (S3.sep.2) `geom-reduced field ext ⇒ separable`.
290	  --   (b.2) `GeometricallyIrreducible (X ↘ Spec k)` ⇒
291	  --         `IsPurelyInseparable k Γ(X, ⊤)`. Reduction chain:
292	  --         X_{\bar k} irreducible ⇒ `Γ(X_{\bar k}, ⊤)` is a domain;
293	  --         combine with the flat-base-change identification
294	  --         `Γ(X_{\bar k}, ⊤) ≅ Γ(X, ⊤) ⊗_k \bar k` (still the gap)
295	  --         ⇒ `Γ ⊗_k \bar k` has a unique minimal prime
296	  --         ⇒ `Γ/k` purely inseparable. Two named structural sub-gaps:
297	  --         (S3.pi.1) `Γ(X_{\bar k}, ⊤) ≅ Γ ⊗_k \bar k` (= step (e)
298	  --         "flat base change of Γ for proper schemes") and (S3.pi.2)
299	  --         "finite-dim algebra over a field with unique minimal
300	  --         prime ⇒ source is purely inseparable".
301	  --
302	  -- Net iter-148 structural reduction: the proof scaffold sets up the
303	  -- algebraic flow `appTop.hom surj ⟺ algebraMap k Γ surj
304	  -- ⟸ IsPurelyInseparable k Γ ∧ Algebra.IsSeparable k Γ`, so iter-149+
305	  -- can target the two named sub-claims directly without re-deriving
306	  -- the algebraic skeleton.
307	  rw [RingHom.range_eq_top]
308	  -- (a) Equip `Γ(X, ⊤)` with a `Field` structure via `_hΓfield`.
309	  letI _hΓfield' : Field ↥(X.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ⊤)) := _hΓfield.toField
310	  -- Define the composition `k → Γ(Spec k, ⊤) → Γ(X, ⊤)` as a
311	  -- `CommRingCat`-morphism. The `(ΓSpecIso (.of k)).inv` arrow is the
312	  -- algebra-map `(.of k) ⟶ Γ(Spec (.of k), ⊤)`.
313	  set α : (CommRingCat.of k) ⟶ X.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ⊤) :=
314	    (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)).inv ≫ (X ↘ Spec (CommRingCat.of k)).appTop with hαdef
315	  -- (a, cont.) Equip `Γ(X, ⊤)` with an `Algebra k`-structure via `α.hom`.
316	  letI algkΓ : Algebra k ↥(X.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ⊤)) := α.hom.toAlgebra
317	  -- The `algebraMap k Γ` literal coincides with `α.hom` by `toAlgebra`.
318	  have h_algebraMap_eq :
319	      (algebraMap k ↥(X.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ⊤))) = α.hom := rfl
320	  -- (a, cont.) Reduce surjectivity of `appTop.hom` to surjectivity of `algebraMap k Γ`.
321	  suffices h_surj : Function.Surjective
322	      (algebraMap k ↥(X.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ⊤))) by
323	    intro y
324	    obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ := h_surj y
325	    refine ⟨(Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)).inv.hom c, ?_⟩
326	    -- `appTop.hom ∘ (ΓSpecIso).inv.hom = α.hom = algebraMap k Γ`.
327	    have hcomp :
328	        ((X ↘ Spec (CommRingCat.of k)).appTop.hom).comp
329	          ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)).inv.hom) = α.hom := by
330	      simp [hαdef, CommRingCat.hom_comp]
331	    have hcompc := congrArg (fun (f : k →+* _) => f c) hcomp
332	    simp only [RingHom.comp_apply] at hcompc
333	    -- `algebraMap k Γ c = y` from `hc`; rewrite `algebraMap` to `α.hom`.
334	    rw [hcompc]
335	    -- Now goal: `α.hom c = y`. By definitional `algebraMap = α.hom`.
336	    have hac : α.hom c = (algebraMap k ↥(X.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ⊤))) c := by
337	      rw [h_algebraMap_eq]
338	    rw [hac]; exact hc
339	  -- (b.1) + (b.2) consolidated structured sorry.
340	  --
341	  -- The product `IsPurelyInseparable k Γ ∧ Algebra.IsSeparable k Γ`
342	  -- comes from the conjunction of:

404	    {C A : Over (Spec (.of k))}
405	    [IsSeparated A.hom]
406	    [IsReduced C.left]

410	      (U.ι : (U : C.left.Opens).toScheme ⟶ C.left) ≫ g.left) :
411	    f = g :=

6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.FieldTheory.IsAlgClosed.AlgebraicClosure
9	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Flat.Basic
10	
11	/-!
12	# Geometrically reduced algebras
13	
14	In this file we introduce geometrically reduced algebras.
15	For a field `k`, we say that a `k`-algebra `A` is geometrically reduced (`IsGeometricallyReduced`)
16	if the tensor product `AlgebraicClosure k ⊗[k] A` is reduced.
17	
18	## Main results
19	- `IsGeometricallyReduced.of_forall_fg`: for a field `k` and a commutative `k`-algebra `A`, if all
20	  finitely generated subalgebras `B` of `A` are geometrically reduced, then `A` is geometrically
21	  reduced.
22	
23	## References
24	- See [https://stacks.math.columbia.edu/tag/05DS] for some theory of geometrically reduced algebras.
25	  Note that their definition differs from the one here, we still need a proof that these are
26	  equivalent (see TODO).
27	
28	## TODO
29	- Prove that if `A` is a geometrically reduced `k`-algebra, then for every field extension `K` of
30	  `k` the `K`-algebra `K ⊗[k] A` is reduced.
31	
32	-/
33	
34	@[expose] public section
35	
36	open TensorProduct
37	
38	noncomputable section
39	
40	namespace Algebra
41	
42	variable {k A : Type*} [Field k] [Ring A] [Algebra k A]
43	
44	/-- The `k`-algebra `A` is geometrically reduced iff its base change to `AlgebraicClosure k` is
45	  reduced. -/
46	@[mk_iff]
47	class IsGeometricallyReduced (k A : Type*) [Field k] [Ring A] [Algebra k A] : Prop where
48	  isReduced_algebraicClosure_tensorProduct : IsReduced (AlgebraicClosure k ⊗[k] A)
49	
50	attribute [instance] IsGeometricallyReduced.isReduced_algebraicClosure_tensorProduct
51	
52	instance (k A K : Type*) [Field k] [Ring A] [Algebra k A] [Field K] [Algebra k K]
53	    [Algebra.IsAlgebraic k K] [IsGeometricallyReduced k A] : IsReduced (K ⊗[k] A) :=
54	  isReduced_of_injective
55	    (Algebra.TensorProduct.map ((IsAlgClosed.lift : K →ₐ[k] AlgebraicClosure k)) 1)
56	    (Module.Flat.rTensor_preserves_injective_linearMap _ (RingHom.injective _))
57	
58	lemma IsGeometricallyReduced.of_injective {B : Type*} [Ring B] [Algebra k B] (f : A →ₐ[k] B)
59	    (hf : Function.Injective f) [IsGeometricallyReduced k B] : IsGeometricallyReduced k A :=
60	  ⟨isReduced_of_injective (Algebra.TensorProduct.map 1 f)
61	    (Module.Flat.lTensor_preserves_injective_linearMap _ hf)⟩
62	
63	variable (k) in
64	theorem isReduced_of_isGeometricallyReduced [IsGeometricallyReduced k A] : IsReduced A :=
65	  isReduced_of_injective
66	    (Algebra.TensorProduct.includeRight : A →ₐ[k] (AlgebraicClosure k) ⊗[k] A)
67	    (Algebra.TensorProduct.includeRight_injective <| FaithfulSMul.algebraMap_injective _ _)
68	
69	/-- If all finitely generated subalgebras of `A` are geometrically reduced, then `A` is
70	  geometrically reduced. -/
71	@[stacks 030T]
72	theorem IsGeometricallyReduced.of_forall_fg
73	    (h : ∀ B : Subalgebra k A, B.FG → IsGeometricallyReduced k B) :
74	    IsGeometricallyReduced k A := by
75	  simp_rw [isGeometricallyReduced_iff] at h
76	  exact ⟨IsReduced.tensorProduct_of_flat_of_forall_fg h⟩
77	
78	end Algebra
79

6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Polynomial.Expand
9	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Polynomial.Splits
10	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Squarefree.Basic
11	public import Mathlib.FieldTheory.IntermediateField.Basic
12	public import Mathlib.FieldTheory.Minpoly.Field
13	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Polynomial.Content
14	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.PowerBasis
15	public import Mathlib.Data.ENat.Lattice
16	
17	/-!
18	
19	# Separable polynomials
20	
21	We define a polynomial to be separable if it is coprime with its derivative. We prove basic
22	properties about separable polynomials here.
23	
24	## Main definitions
25	
26	* `Polynomial.Separable f`: a polynomial `f` is separable iff it is coprime with its derivative.
27	* `IsSeparable K x`: an element `x` is separable over `K` iff the minimal polynomial of `x`
28	  over `K` is separable.
29	* `Algebra.IsSeparable K L`: `L` is separable over `K` iff every element in `L` is separable
30	  over `K`.
31	
32	-/
33	
34	@[expose] public section
35	
36	
37	universe u v w
38	
39	open Polynomial Finset
40	
41	namespace Polynomial
42	
43	section CommSemiring
44	
45	variable {R : Type u} [CommSemiring R] {S : Type v} [CommSemiring S]
46	
47	/-- A polynomial is separable iff it is coprime with its derivative. -/
48	@[stacks 09H1 "first part"]
49	def Separable (f : R[X]) : Prop :=
50	  IsCoprime f (derivative f)
51	
52	theorem separable_def (f : R[X]) : f.Separable ↔ IsCoprime f (derivative f) :=
53	  Iff.rfl
54	
55	theorem separable_def' (f : R[X]) : f.Separable ↔ ∃ a b : R[X], a * f + b * (derivative f) = 1 :=
56	  Iff.rfl
57	
58	theorem not_separable_zero [Nontrivial R] : ¬Separable (0 : R[X]) := by
59	  rintro ⟨x, y, h⟩
60	  simp only [derivative_zero, mul_zero, add_zero, zero_ne_one] at h
61	
62	theorem Separable.ne_zero [Nontrivial R] {f : R[X]} (h : f.Separable) : f ≠ 0 :=
63	  (not_separable_zero <| · ▸ h)
64	
65	@[simp]
66	theorem separable_one : (1 : R[X]).Separable :=
67	  isCoprime_one_left
68	
69	@[nontriviality]
70	theorem separable_of_subsingleton [Subsingleton R] (f : R[X]) : f.Separable := by
71	  simp [Separable, IsCoprime, eq_iff_true_of_subsingleton]
72	
73	theorem separable_X_add_C (a : R) : (X + C a).Separable := by
74	  rw [separable_def, derivative_add, derivative_X, derivative_C, add_zero]
75	  exact isCoprime_one_right
76	
77	theorem separable_X : (X : R[X]).Separable := by
78	  rw [separable_def, derivative_X]
79	  exact isCoprime_one_right
80	
81	theorem separable_C (r : R) : (C r).Separable ↔ IsUnit r := by
82	  rw [separable_def, derivative_C, isCoprime_zero_right, isUnit_C]
83	
84	theorem Separable.of_mul_left {f g : R[X]} (h : (f * g).Separable) : f.Separable := by
85	  have := h.of_mul_left_left; rw [derivative_mul] at this
86	  exact IsCoprime.of_mul_right_left (IsCoprime.of_add_mul_left_right this)
87	
88	theorem Separable.of_mul_right {f g : R[X]} (h : (f * g).Separable) : g.Separable := by
89	  rw [mul_comm] at h
90	  exact h.of_mul_left
91	
92	theorem Separable.of_dvd {f g : R[X]} (hf : f.Separable) (hfg : g ∣ f) : g.Separable := by
93	  rcases hfg with ⟨f', rfl⟩
94	  exact Separable.of_mul_left hf
95	
96	theorem separable_gcd_left {F : Type*} [Field F] [DecidableEq F[X]]
97	    {f : F[X]} (hf : f.Separable) (g : F[X]) :
98	    (EuclideanDomain.gcd f g).Separable :=
99	  Separable.of_dvd hf (EuclideanDomain.gcd_dvd_left f g)
100

6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.Algebra.CharP.IntermediateField
9	public import Mathlib.FieldTheory.SeparableClosure
10	
11	/-!
12	
13	# Basic results about purely inseparable extensions
14	
15	This file contains basic definitions and results about purely inseparable extensions.
16	
17	## Main definitions
18	
19	- `IsPurelyInseparable`: typeclass for purely inseparable field extensions: an algebraic extension
20	  `E / F` is purely inseparable if and only if the minimal polynomial of every element of `E ∖ F`
21	  is not separable.
22	
23	## Main results
24	
25	- `IsPurelyInseparable.surjective_algebraMap_of_isSeparable`,
26	  `IsPurelyInseparable.bijective_algebraMap_of_isSeparable`,
27	  `IntermediateField.eq_bot_of_isPurelyInseparable_of_isSeparable`:
28	  if `E / F` is both purely inseparable and separable, then `algebraMap F E` is surjective
29	  (hence bijective). In particular, if an intermediate field of `E / F` is both purely inseparable
30	  and separable, then it is equal to `F`.
31	
32	- `isPurelyInseparable_iff_pow_mem`: a field extension `E / F` of exponential characteristic `q` is
33	  purely inseparable if and only if for every element `x` of `E`, there exists a natural number `n`
34	  such that `x ^ (q ^ n)` is contained in `F`.
35	
36	- `IsPurelyInseparable.trans`: if `E / F` and `K / E` are both purely inseparable extensions, then
37	  `K / F` is also purely inseparable.
38	
39	- `isPurelyInseparable_iff_natSepDegree_eq_one`: `E / F` is purely inseparable if and only if for
40	  every element `x` of `E`, its minimal polynomial has separable degree one.
41	
42	- `isPurelyInseparable_iff_minpoly_eq_X_pow_sub_C`: a field extension `E / F` of exponential
43	  characteristic `q` is purely inseparable if and only if for every element `x` of `E`, the minimal
44	  polynomial of `x` over `F` is of form `X ^ (q ^ n) - y` for some natural number `n` and some
45	  element `y` of `F`.
46	
47	- `isPurelyInseparable_iff_minpoly_eq_X_sub_C_pow`: a field extension `E / F` of exponential
48	  characteristic `q` is purely inseparable if and only if for every element `x` of `E`, the minimal
49	  polynomial of `x` over `F` is of form `(X - x) ^ (q ^ n)` for some natural number `n`.
50	
51	- `isPurelyInseparable_iff_finSepDegree_eq_one`: an extension is purely inseparable
52	  if and only if it has finite separable degree (`Field.finSepDegree`) one.
53	
54	- `IsPurelyInseparable.normal`: a purely inseparable extension is normal.
55	
56	- `separableClosure.isPurelyInseparable`: if `E / F` is algebraic, then `E` is purely inseparable
57	  over the separable closure of `F` in `E`.
58	
59	- `separableClosure_le_iff`: if `E / F` is algebraic, then an intermediate field of `E / F` contains
60	  the separable closure of `F` in `E` if and only if `E` is purely inseparable over it.
61	
62	- `eq_separableClosure_iff`: if `E / F` is algebraic, then an intermediate field of `E / F` is equal
63	  to the separable closure of `F` in `E` if and only if it is separable over `F`, and `E`
64	  is purely inseparable over it.
65	
66	- `IsPurelyInseparable.injective_comp_algebraMap`: if `E / F` is purely inseparable, then for any
67	  reduced ring `L`, the map `(E →+* L) → (F →+* L)` induced by `algebraMap F E` is injective.
68	  In particular, a purely inseparable field extension is an epimorphism in the category of fields.
69	
70	- `IsPurelyInseparable.of_injective_comp_algebraMap`: if `L` is an algebraically closed field
71	  containing `E`, such that the map `(E →+* L) → (F →+* L)` induced by `algebraMap F E` is
72	  injective, then `E / F` is purely inseparable. As a corollary, epimorphisms in the category of
73	  fields must be purely inseparable extensions.
74	
75	- `Field.finSepDegree_eq`: if `E / F` is algebraic, then the `Field.finSepDegree F E` is equal to
76	  `Field.sepDegree F E` as a natural number. This means that the cardinality of `Field.Emb F E`
77	  and the degree of `(separableClosure F E) / F` are both finite or infinite, and when they are
78	  finite, they coincide.
79	
80	- `Field.finSepDegree_mul_finInsepDegree`: the finite separable degree multiply by the finite
81	  inseparable degree is equal to the (finite) field extension degree.
82	
83	## Tags
84	
85	separable degree, degree, separable closure, purely inseparable
86	
87	-/
88	
89	@[expose] public section
90	
91	open Module Polynomial IntermediateField Field
92	
93	noncomputable section
94	
95	universe u v w
96	
97	section General
98	
99	variable (F E : Type*) [CommRing F] [Ring E] [Algebra F E]
100	variable (K : Type*) [Ring K] [Algebra F K]

13	# Proper morphisms
14	
15	A morphism of schemes is proper if it is separated, universally closed and (locally) of finite type.
16	Note that we don't require quasi-compact, since this is implied by universally closed.

18	## Main results
19	- `AlgebraicGeometry.isField_of_universallyClosed`:
20	  If `X` is an integral scheme that is universally closed over `Spec K`, then `Γ(X, ⊤)` is a field.
21	- `AlgebraicGeometry.finite_appTop_of_universallyClosed`:
22	  If `X` is an integral scheme that is universally closed and of finite type over `Spec K`,
23	  then `Γ(X, ⊤)` is finite dimensional over `K`.

40	/-- A morphism is proper if it is separated, universally closed and locally of finite type. -/
41	@[mk_iff]
42	class IsProper : Prop extends IsSeparated f, UniversallyClosed f, LocallyOfFiniteType f where
43	
44	lemma isProper_eq : @IsProper =
45	    (@IsSeparated ⊓ @UniversallyClosed : MorphismProperty Scheme) ⊓ @LocallyOfFiniteType := by
46	  ext X Y f

48	  rfl

58	  infer_instance

62	  infer_instance

64	instance [IsProper f] [IsProper g] : IsProper (f ≫ g) where
65

13	# Geometrically Reduced Schemes
14	
15	## Main results
16	- `AlgebraicGeometry.GeometricallyReduced`:
17	  We say that morphism `f : X ⟶ Y` is geometrically reduced if for all `Spec K ⟶ Y` with `K`
18	  a field, `X ×[Y] Spec K` is reduced.
19	  We also provide the fact that this is stable under base change (by infer_instance)
20	- `GeometricallyReduced.iff_geometricallyReduced_fiber`:
21	  A scheme is geometrically reduced over `S` iff the fibers of all
22	  `s : S` are geometrically reduced.
23	- `AlgebraicGeometry.GeometricallyReduced.isReduced_of_flat_of_isLocallyNoetherian`:
24	  If `X` is geometrically reduced and flat over a reduced and locally noetherian scheme,
25	  then `X` is also reduced.
26	  In particular, the base change of a geometrically reduced and flat scheme to an
27	  reduced and locally noetherian scheme is reduced (by infer_instance).
28	
29	## TODO
30	Get rid of the noetherian assumption.
31	-/

45	  geometrically_isReduced : geometrically IsReduced f

17	/-!
18	
19	# Smooth morphisms
20	
21	In this file we define smooth morphisms. The main definitions are:
22	
23	- `AlgebraicGeometry.Smooth`: A morphism of schemes `f : X ⟶ Y` is smooth if for each affine `U ⊆ Y`
24	  and `V ⊆ f ⁻¹' U`, the induced map `Γ(Y, U) ⟶ Γ(X, V)` is smooth.
25	
26	- `AlgebraicGeometry.SmoothOfRelativeDimension`: A morphism of schemes `f : X ⟶ Y` is smooth of
27	  relative dimension `n` if for each `x : X` there exists an affine open neighborhood `V` of `x`
28	  and an affine open neighborhood `U` of `f.base x` with `V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U` such that the induced
29	  map `Γ(Y, U) ⟶ Γ(X, V)` is standard smooth (of relative dimension `n`).
30	
31	## Main results
32	
33	- `AlgebraicGeometry.Smooth.iff_forall_exists_isStandardSmooth`: A morphism of schemes is smooth
34	  if and only if for each `x : X` there exists an affine open neighborhood `V` of `x`
35	  and an affine open neighborhood `U` of `f.base x` with `V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U` such that the induced
36	  map `Γ(Y, U) ⟶ Γ(X, V)` is standard smooth.
37	
38	## Notes
39	
40	This contribution was created as part of the AIM workshop "Formalizing algebraic geometry" in
41	June 2024.
42	
43	-/

17	/-!
18	
19	# Separable polynomials
20	
21	We define a polynomial to be separable if it is coprime with its derivative. We prove basic
22	properties about separable polynomials here.
23	
24	## Main definitions
25	
26	* `Polynomial.Separable f`: a polynomial `f` is separable iff it is coprime with its derivative.
27	* `IsSeparable K x`: an element `x` is separable over `K` iff the minimal polynomial of `x`
28	  over `K` is separable.
29	* `Algebra.IsSeparable K L`: `L` is separable over `K` iff every element in `L` is separable
30	  over `K`.
31	
32	-/
33	
34	@[expose] public section
35	
36	
37	universe u v w
38	
39	open Polynomial Finset
40	
41	namespace Polynomial
42	
43	section CommSemiring
44	
45	variable {R : Type u} [CommSemiring R] {S : Type v} [CommSemiring S]
46	
47	/-- A polynomial is separable iff it is coprime with its derivative. -/
48	@[stacks 09H1 "first part"]
49	def Separable (f : R[X]) : Prop :=
50	  IsCoprime f (derivative f)
51	
52	theorem separable_def (f : R[X]) : f.Separable ↔ IsCoprime f (derivative f) :=
53	  Iff.rfl
54	
55	theorem separable_def' (f : R[X]) : f.Separable ↔ ∃ a b : R[X], a * f + b * (derivative f) = 1 :=
56	  Iff.rfl
57	
58	theorem not_separable_zero [Nontrivial R] : ¬Separable (0 : R[X]) := by
59	  rintro ⟨x, y, h⟩
60	  simp only [derivative_zero, mul_zero, add_zero, zero_ne_one] at h
61	
62	theorem Separable.ne_zero [Nontrivial R] {f : R[X]} (h : f.Separable) : f ≠ 0 :=
63	  (not_separable_zero <| · ▸ h)
64	
65	@[simp]
66	theorem separable_one : (1 : R[X]).Separable :=
67	  isCoprime_one_left
68	
69	@[nontriviality]
70	theorem separable_of_subsingleton [Subsingleton R] (f : R[X]) : f.Separable := by
71	  simp [Separable, IsCoprime, eq_iff_true_of_subsingleton]
72	
73	theorem separable_X_add_C (a : R) : (X + C a).Separable := by
74	  rw [separable_def, derivative_add, derivative_X, derivative_C, add_zero]
75	  exact isCoprime_one_right
76	
77	theorem separable_X : (X : R[X]).Separable := by
78	  rw [separable_def, derivative_X]
79	  exact isCoprime_one_right
80	
81	theorem separable_C (r : R) : (C r).Separable ↔ IsUnit r := by
82	  rw [separable_def, derivative_C, isCoprime_zero_right, isUnit_C]
83	
84	theorem Separable.of_mul_left {f g : R[X]} (h : (f * g).Separable) : f.Separable := by
85	  have := h.of_mul_left_left; rw [derivative_mul] at this
86	  exact IsCoprime.of_mul_right_left (IsCoprime.of_add_mul_left_right this)
87	
88	theorem Separable.of_mul_right {f g : R[X]} (h : (f * g).Separable) : g.Separable := by
89	  rw [mul_comm] at h
90	  exact h.of_mul_left
91	
92	theorem Separable.of_dvd {f g : R[X]} (hf : f.Separable) (hfg : g ∣ f) : g.Separable := by
93	  rcases hfg with ⟨f', rfl⟩
94	  exact Separable.of_mul_left hf
95	
96	theorem separable_gcd_left {F : Type*} [Field F] [DecidableEq F[X]]
97	    {f : F[X]} (hf : f.Separable) (g : F[X]) :
98	    (EuclideanDomain.gcd f g).Separable :=
99	  Separable.of_dvd hf (EuclideanDomain.gcd_dvd_left f g)
100	
101	theorem separable_gcd_right {F : Type*} [Field F] [DecidableEq F[X]]
102	    {g : F[X]} (f : F[X]) (hg : g.Separable) :
103	    (EuclideanDomain.gcd f g).Separable :=
104	  Separable.of_dvd hg (EuclideanDomain.gcd_dvd_right f g)
105	
106	theorem Separable.isCoprime {f g : R[X]} (h : (f * g).Separable) : IsCoprime f g := by
107	  have := h.of_mul_left_left; rw [derivative_mul] at this
108	  exact IsCoprime.of_mul_right_right (IsCoprime.of_add_mul_left_right this)
109	
110	theorem Separable.of_pow' {f : R[X]} :
111	    ∀ {n : ℕ} (_h : (f ^ n).Separable), IsUnit f ∨ f.Separable ∧ n = 1 ∨ n = 0
112	  | 0 => fun _h => Or.inr <| Or.inr rfl
113	  | 1 => fun h => Or.inr <| Or.inl ⟨pow_one f ▸ h, rfl⟩
114	  | n + 2 => fun h => by
115	    rw [pow_succ, pow_succ] at h
116	    exact Or.inl (isCoprime_self.1 h.isCoprime.of_mul_left_right)
117	
118	theorem Separable.of_pow {f : R[X]} (hf : ¬IsUnit f) {n : ℕ} (hn : n ≠ 0)
119	    (hfs : (f ^ n).Separable) : f.Separable ∧ n = 1 :=
120	  (hfs.of_pow'.resolve_left hf).resolve_right hn
121	
122	theorem Separable.map {p : R[X]} (h : p.Separable) {f : R →+* S} : (p.map f).Separable :=
123	  let ⟨a, b, H⟩ := h
124	  ⟨a.map f, b.map f, by
125	    rw [derivative_map, ← Polynomial.map_mul, ← Polynomial.map_mul, ← Polynomial.map_add, H,
126	      Polynomial.map_one]⟩
127	
128	theorem _root_.Associated.separable {f g : R[X]}
129	    (ha : Associated f g) (h : f.Separable) : g.Separable := by
130	  grind [Separable.of_dvd, Associated.dvd']
131	
132	theorem _root_.Associated.separable_iff {f g : R[X]}
133	    (ha : Associated f g) : f.Separable ↔ g.Separable := ⟨ha.separable, ha.symm.separable⟩
134	
135	theorem Separable.mul_unit {f g : R[X]} (hf : f.Separable) (hg : IsUnit g) : (f * g).Separable :=
136	  (associated_mul_unit_right f g hg).separable hf
137	
138	theorem Separable.unit_mul {f g : R[X]} (hf : IsUnit f) (hg : g.Separable) : (f * g).Separable :=
139	  (associated_unit_mul_right g f hf).separable hg
140

9	public import Mathlib.FieldTheory.SeparableClosure
10	
11	/-!
12	
13	# Basic results about purely inseparable extensions
14	
15	This file contains basic definitions and results about purely inseparable extensions.
16	
17	## Main definitions
18	
19	- `IsPurelyInseparable`: typeclass for purely inseparable field extensions: an algebraic extension
20	  `E / F` is purely inseparable if and only if the minimal polynomial of every element of `E ∖ F`
21	  is not separable.
22	
23	## Main results
24	
25	- `IsPurelyInseparable.surjective_algebraMap_of_isSeparable`,
26	  `IsPurelyInseparable.bijective_algebraMap_of_isSeparable`,
27	  `IntermediateField.eq_bot_of_isPurelyInseparable_of_isSeparable`:
28	  if `E / F` is both purely inseparable and separable, then `algebraMap F E` is surjective
29	  (hence bijective). In particular, if an intermediate field of `E / F` is both purely inseparable
30	  and separable, then it is equal to `F`.
31	
32	- `isPurelyInseparable_iff_pow_mem`: a field extension `E / F` of exponential characteristic `q` is
33	  purely inseparable if and only if for every element `x` of `E`, there exists a natural number `n`
34	  such that `x ^ (q ^ n)` is contained in `F`.
35	
36	- `IsPurelyInseparable.trans`: if `E / F` and `K / E` are both purely inseparable extensions, then
37	  `K / F` is also purely inseparable.
38	
39	- `isPurelyInseparable_iff_natSepDegree_eq_one`: `E / F` is purely inseparable if and only if for
40	  every element `x` of `E`, its minimal polynomial has separable degree one.
41	
42	- `isPurelyInseparable_iff_minpoly_eq_X_pow_sub_C`: a field extension `E / F` of exponential
43	  characteristic `q` is purely inseparable if and only if for every element `x` of `E`, the minimal
44	  polynomial of `x` over `F` is of form `X ^ (q ^ n) - y` for some natural number `n` and some
45	  element `y` of `F`.
46	
47	- `isPurelyInseparable_iff_minpoly_eq_X_sub_C_pow`: a field extension `E / F` of exponential
48	  characteristic `q` is purely inseparable if and only if for every element `x` of `E`, the minimal
49	  polynomial of `x` over `F` is of form `(X - x) ^ (q ^ n)` for some natural number `n`.
50	
51	- `isPurelyInseparable_iff_finSepDegree_eq_one`: an extension is purely inseparable
52	  if and only if it has finite separable degree (`Field.finSepDegree`) one.
53	
54	- `IsPurelyInseparable.normal`: a purely inseparable extension is normal.
55	
56	- `separableClosure.isPurelyInseparable`: if `E / F` is algebraic, then `E` is purely inseparable
57	  over the separable closure of `F` in `E`.
58	
59	- `separableClosure_le_iff`: if `E / F` is algebraic, then an intermediate field of `E / F` contains
60	  the separable closure of `F` in `E` if and only if `E` is purely inseparable over it.
61	
62	- `eq_separableClosure_iff`: if `E / F` is algebraic, then an intermediate field of `E / F` is equal
63	  to the separable closure of `F` in `E` if and only if it is separable over `F`, and `E`
64	  is purely inseparable over it.
65	
66	- `IsPurelyInseparable.injective_comp_algebraMap`: if `E / F` is purely inseparable, then for any
67	  reduced ring `L`, the map `(E →+* L) → (F →+* L)` induced by `algebraMap F E` is injective.
68	  In particular, a purely inseparable field extension is an epimorphism in the category of fields.
69	
70	- `IsPurelyInseparable.of_injective_comp_algebraMap`: if `L` is an algebraically closed field
71	  containing `E`, such that the map `(E →+* L) → (F →+* L)` induced by `algebraMap F E` is
72	  injective, then `E / F` is purely inseparable. As a corollary, epimorphisms in the category of
73	  fields must be purely inseparable extensions.
74	
75	- `Field.finSepDegree_eq`: if `E / F` is algebraic, then the `Field.finSepDegree F E` is equal to
76	  `Field.sepDegree F E` as a natural number. This means that the cardinality of `Field.Emb F E`
77	  and the degree of `(separableClosure F E) / F` are both finite or infinite, and when they are
78	  finite, they coincide.
79	
80	- `Field.finSepDegree_mul_finInsepDegree`: the finite separable degree multiply by the finite
81	  inseparable degree is equal to the (finite) field extension degree.
82	
83	## Tags
84	
85	separable degree, degree, separable closure, purely inseparable
86	
87	-/

99	variable (F E : Type*) [CommRing F] [Ring E] [Algebra F E]
100	variable (K : Type*) [Ring K] [Algebra F K]
101	
102	/-- Typeclass for purely inseparable field extensions: an algebraic extension `E / F` is purely
103	inseparable if and only if the minimal polynomial of every element of `E ∖ F` is not separable.
104	
105	We define this for general (commutative) rings and only assume `F` and `E` are fields
106	if this is needed for a proof. -/
107	class IsPurelyInseparable : Prop where
108	  isIntegral : Algebra.IsIntegral F E
109	  inseparable' (x : E) : IsSeparable F x → x ∈ (algebraMap F E).range
110	
111	attribute [instance] IsPurelyInseparable.isIntegral
112	
113	variable {E} in
114	theorem IsPurelyInseparable.isIntegral' [IsPurelyInseparable F E] (x : E) : IsIntegral F x :=
115	  Algebra.IsIntegral.isIntegral _
116	
117	theorem IsPurelyInseparable.isAlgebraic [Nontrivial F] [IsPurelyInseparable F E] :
118	    Algebra.IsAlgebraic F E := inferInstance
119	
120	variable {E}
121	
122	theorem IsPurelyInseparable.inseparable [IsPurelyInseparable F E] :
123	    ∀ x : E, IsSeparable F x → x ∈ (algebraMap F E).range :=
124	  IsPurelyInseparable.inseparable'
125	
126	variable {F}
127	
128	theorem isPurelyInseparable_iff : IsPurelyInseparable F E ↔ ∀ x : E,
129	    IsIntegral F x ∧ (IsSeparable F x → x ∈ (algebraMap F E).range) :=
130	  ⟨fun h x ↦ ⟨h.isIntegral' _ x, h.inseparable' x⟩, fun h ↦ ⟨⟨fun x ↦ (h x).1⟩, fun x ↦ (h x).2⟩⟩
131	
132	variable {K}
133	
134	/-- Transfer `IsPurelyInseparable` across an `AlgEquiv`. -/
135	theorem AlgEquiv.isPurelyInseparable (e : K ≃ₐ[F] E) [IsPurelyInseparable F K] :
136	    IsPurelyInseparable F E := by
137	  refine ⟨⟨fun _ ↦ by rw [← isIntegral_algEquiv e.symm]; exact IsPurelyInseparable.isIntegral' F _⟩,
138	    fun x h ↦ ?_⟩
139	  rw [IsSeparable, ← minpoly.algEquiv_eq e.symm] at h
140	  simpa only [RingHom.mem_range, algebraMap_eq_apply] using IsPurelyInseparable.inseparable F _ h
141	
142	theorem AlgEquiv.isPurelyInseparable_iff (e : K ≃ₐ[F] E) :
143	    IsPurelyInseparable F K ↔ IsPurelyInseparable F E :=
144	  ⟨fun _ ↦ e.isPurelyInseparable, fun _ ↦ e.symm.isPurelyInseparable⟩
145	
146	/-- If `E / F` is an algebraic extension, `F` is separably closed,
147	then `E / F` is purely inseparable. -/
148	instance Algebra.IsAlgebraic.isPurelyInseparable_of_isSepClosed
149	    {F : Type u} {E : Type v} [Field F] [Ring E] [IsDomain E] [Algebra F E]
150	    [Algebra.IsAlgebraic F E] [IsSepClosed F] : IsPurelyInseparable F E :=
151	  ⟨inferInstance, fun x h ↦ minpoly.mem_range_of_degree_eq_one F x <|
152	    IsSepClosed.degree_eq_one_of_irreducible F (minpoly.irreducible
153	      (Algebra.IsIntegral.isIntegral _)) h⟩
154	
155	variable (F E K)
156	
157	/-- If `E / F` is both purely inseparable and separable, then `algebraMap F E` is surjective. -/
158	theorem IsPurelyInseparable.surjective_algebraMap_of_isSeparable
159	    [IsPurelyInseparable F E] [Algebra.IsSeparable F E] : Function.Surjective (algebraMap F E) :=
160	  fun x ↦ IsPurelyInseparable.inseparable F x (Algebra.IsSeparable.isSeparable F x)
161	
162	/-- If `E / F` is both purely inseparable and separable, then `algebraMap F E` is bijective. -/
163	theorem IsPurelyInseparable.bijective_algebraMap_of_isSeparable
164	    [Nontrivial E] [IsDomain F] [IsTorsionFree F E]
165	    [IsPurelyInseparable F E] [Algebra.IsSeparable F E] : Function.Bijective (algebraMap F E) :=
166	  ⟨FaithfulSMul.algebraMap_injective F E, surjective_algebraMap_of_isSeparable F E⟩
167	
168	variable {F E} in
169	/-- If a subalgebra of `E / F` is both purely inseparable and separable, then it is equal
170	to `F`. -/
171	theorem Subalgebra.eq_bot_of_isPurelyInseparable_of_isSeparable (L : Subalgebra F E)
172	    [IsPurelyInseparable F L] [Algebra.IsSeparable F L] : L = ⊥ := bot_unique fun x hx ↦ by
173	  obtain ⟨y, hy⟩ := IsPurelyInseparable.surjective_algebraMap_of_isSeparable F L ⟨x, hx⟩
174	  exact ⟨y, congr_arg (Subalgebra.val _) hy⟩
175	
176	/-- If an intermediate field of `E / F` is both purely inseparable and separable, then it is equal
177	to `F`. -/
178	theorem IntermediateField.eq_bot_of_isPurelyInseparable_of_isSeparable
179	    {F : Type u} {E : Type v} [Field F] [Field E] [Algebra F E] (L : IntermediateField F E)
180	    [IsPurelyInseparable F L] [Algebra.IsSeparable F L] : L = ⊥ := bot_unique fun x hx ↦ by
181	  obtain ⟨y, hy⟩ := IsPurelyInseparable.surjective_algebraMap_of_isSeparable F L ⟨x, hx⟩
182	  exact ⟨y, congr_arg (algebraMap L E) hy⟩
183	
184	/-- If `E / F` is purely inseparable, then the separable closure of `F` in `E` is
185	equal to `F`. -/
186	theorem separableClosure.eq_bot_of_isPurelyInseparable
187	    (F : Type u) (E : Type v) [Field F] [Field E] [Algebra F E] [IsPurelyInseparable F E] :
188	    separableClosure F E = ⊥ :=
189	  bot_unique fun x h ↦ IsPurelyInseparable.inseparable F x (mem_separableClosure_iff.1 h)
190	
191	/-- If `E / F` is an algebraic extension, then the separable closure of `F` in `E` is
192	equal to `F` if and only if `E / F` is purely inseparable. -/
193	theorem separableClosure.eq_bot_iff
194	    {F : Type u} {E : Type v} [Field F] [Field E] [Algebra F E] [Algebra.IsAlgebraic F E] :
195	    separableClosure F E = ⊥ ↔ IsPurelyInseparable F E :=
196	  ⟨fun h ↦ isPurelyInseparable_iff.2 fun x ↦ ⟨Algebra.IsIntegral.isIntegral x, fun hs ↦ by
197	    simpa only [h] using mem_separableClosure_iff.2 hs⟩, fun _ ↦ eq_bot_of_isPurelyInseparable F E⟩
198	
199	instance isPurelyInseparable_self : IsPurelyInseparable F F :=
200	  ⟨inferInstance, fun x _ ↦ ⟨x, rfl⟩⟩

582	theorem Algebra.isSeparable_iff :
583	    Algebra.IsSeparable F K ↔ ∀ x : K, IsIntegral F x ∧ IsSeparable F x :=
584	  ⟨fun _ x => ⟨Algebra.IsSeparable.isIntegral F x, Algebra.IsSeparable.isSeparable F x⟩,
585	    fun h => ⟨fun x => (h x).2⟩⟩

589	lemma isSeparable_map_iff [Ring L] [Algebra F L] {x : K} (f : K →ₐ[F] L)
590	    (hf : Function.Injective f) : IsSeparable F (f x) ↔ IsSeparable F x := by
591	  simp_rw [IsSeparable, minpoly.algHom_eq _ hf]
592	
593	lemma IsSeparable.map [Ring L] [Algebra F L] {x : K} (f : K →ₐ[F] L) (hf : Function.Injective f)
594	    (H : IsSeparable F x) : IsSeparable F (f x) :=
595	  (isSeparable_map_iff f hf).mpr H
596	
597	lemma Subalgebra.isSeparable_iff [Ring L] [Algebra F L] {S : Subalgebra F L} :
598	    Algebra.IsSeparable F S ↔ ∀ x ∈ S, IsSeparable F x := by
599	  simp_rw [Algebra.isSeparable_def, Subtype.forall,
600	    ← isSeparable_map_iff S.val Subtype.val_injective, coe_val]

6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.FieldTheory.IsAlgClosed.AlgebraicClosure
9	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Flat.Basic
10	
11	/-!
12	# Geometrically reduced algebras
13	
14	In this file we introduce geometrically reduced algebras.
15	For a field `k`, we say that a `k`-algebra `A` is geometrically reduced (`IsGeometricallyReduced`)
16	if the tensor product `AlgebraicClosure k ⊗[k] A` is reduced.
17	
18	## Main results
19	- `IsGeometricallyReduced.of_forall_fg`: for a field `k` and a commutative `k`-algebra `A`, if all
20	  finitely generated subalgebras `B` of `A` are geometrically reduced, then `A` is geometrically
21	  reduced.
22	
23	## References
24	- See [https://stacks.math.columbia.edu/tag/05DS] for some theory of geometrically reduced algebras.
25	  Note that their definition differs from the one here, we still need a proof that these are
26	  equivalent (see TODO).
27	
28	## TODO
29	- Prove that if `A` is a geometrically reduced `k`-algebra, then for every field extension `K` of
30	  `k` the `K`-algebra `K ⊗[k] A` is reduced.
31	
32	-/
33	
34	@[expose] public section
35	
36	open TensorProduct
37	
38	noncomputable section
39	
40	namespace Algebra
41	
42	variable {k A : Type*} [Field k] [Ring A] [Algebra k A]
43	
44	/-- The `k`-algebra `A` is geometrically reduced iff its base change to `AlgebraicClosure k` is
45	  reduced. -/
46	@[mk_iff]
47	class IsGeometricallyReduced (k A : Type*) [Field k] [Ring A] [Algebra k A] : Prop where
48	  isReduced_algebraicClosure_tensorProduct : IsReduced (AlgebraicClosure k ⊗[k] A)
49	
50	attribute [instance] IsGeometricallyReduced.isReduced_algebraicClosure_tensorProduct
51	
52	instance (k A K : Type*) [Field k] [Ring A] [Algebra k A] [Field K] [Algebra k K]
53	    [Algebra.IsAlgebraic k K] [IsGeometricallyReduced k A] : IsReduced (K ⊗[k] A) :=
54	  isReduced_of_injective
55	    (Algebra.TensorProduct.map ((IsAlgClosed.lift : K →ₐ[k] AlgebraicClosure k)) 1)
56	    (Module.Flat.rTensor_preserves_injective_linearMap _ (RingHom.injective _))
57	
58	lemma IsGeometricallyReduced.of_injective {B : Type*} [Ring B] [Algebra k B] (f : A →ₐ[k] B)
59	    (hf : Function.Injective f) [IsGeometricallyReduced k B] : IsGeometricallyReduced k A :=
60	  ⟨isReduced_of_injective (Algebra.TensorProduct.map 1 f)
61	    (Module.Flat.lTensor_preserves_injective_linearMap _ hf)⟩
62	
63	variable (k) in
64	theorem isReduced_of_isGeometricallyReduced [IsGeometricallyReduced k A] : IsReduced A :=
65	  isReduced_of_injective
66	    (Algebra.TensorProduct.includeRight : A →ₐ[k] (AlgebraicClosure k) ⊗[k] A)
67	    (Algebra.TensorProduct.includeRight_injective <| FaithfulSMul.algebraMap_injective _ _)
68	
69	/-- If all finitely generated subalgebras of `A` are geometrically reduced, then `A` is
70	  geometrically reduced. -/
71	@[stacks 030T]
72	theorem IsGeometricallyReduced.of_forall_fg
73	    (h : ∀ B : Subalgebra k A, B.FG → IsGeometricallyReduced k B) :
74	    IsGeometricallyReduced k A := by
75	  simp_rw [isGeometricallyReduced_iff] at h
76	  exact ⟨IsReduced.tensorProduct_of_flat_of_forall_fg h⟩
77	
78	end Algebra
79

12	/-!
13	# Geometrically Reduced Schemes
14	
15	## Main results
16	- `AlgebraicGeometry.GeometricallyReduced`:
17	  We say that morphism `f : X ⟶ Y` is geometrically reduced if for all `Spec K ⟶ Y` with `K`
18	  a field, `X ×[Y] Spec K` is reduced.
19	  We also provide the fact that this is stable under base change (by infer_instance)
20	- `GeometricallyReduced.iff_geometricallyReduced_fiber`:
21	  A scheme is geometrically reduced over `S` iff the fibers of all
22	  `s : S` are geometrically reduced.
23	- `AlgebraicGeometry.GeometricallyReduced.isReduced_of_flat_of_isLocallyNoetherian`:
24	  If `X` is geometrically reduced and flat over a reduced and locally noetherian scheme,
25	  then `X` is also reduced.
26	  In particular, the base change of a geometrically reduced and flat scheme to an
27	  reduced and locally noetherian scheme is reduced (by infer_instance).
28	
29	## TODO
30	Get rid of the noetherian assumption.
31	-/
32	
33	@[expose] public section
34	
35	open CategoryTheory MorphismProperty Limits
36	
37	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
38	
39	variable {X Y Z S : Scheme} (f : X ⟶ S) (g : Y ⟶ S)
40	
41	/-- We say that morphism `f : X ⟶ Y` is geometrically reduced if for all `Spec K ⟶ Y` with `K`
42	a field, `X ×[Y] Spec K` is reduced. -/
43	@[mk_iff]
44	class GeometricallyReduced (f : X ⟶ Y) : Prop where
45	  geometrically_isReduced : geometrically IsReduced f
46	
47	lemma GeometricallyReduced.eq_geometrically :
48	    @GeometricallyReduced = geometrically IsReduced := by
49	  ext; exact geometricallyReduced_iff _
50	
51	instance : IsStableUnderBaseChange @GeometricallyReduced :=
52	  GeometricallyReduced.eq_geometrically ▸ inferInstance
53	
54	instance [GeometricallyReduced g] : GeometricallyReduced (pullback.fst f g) :=
55	  MorphismProperty.pullback_fst f g inferInstance
56	
57	instance [GeometricallyReduced f] : GeometricallyReduced (pullback.snd f g) :=
58	  MorphismProperty.pullback_snd f g inferInstance
59	
60	instance (V : S.Opens) [GeometricallyReduced f] : GeometricallyReduced (f ∣_ V) :=
61	  MorphismProperty.of_isPullback (isPullback_morphismRestrict ..).flip ‹_›
62	
63	instance (s : S) [GeometricallyReduced f] :
64	    GeometricallyReduced (f.fiberToSpecResidueField s) :=
65	  MorphismProperty.pullback_snd _ _ inferInstance
66	
67	instance (s : S) [GeometricallyReduced f] : IsReduced (f.fiber s) :=
68	  GeometricallyReduced.geometrically_isReduced _ _ _ (.of_hasPullback _ _)
69	
70	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
71	lemma GeometricallyReduced.isReduced_of_flat_of_finite_irreducibleComponents
72	    (f : X ⟶ Y) [GeometricallyReduced f] [Flat f]
73	    [IsReduced Y] [Finite (irreducibleComponents Y)] : IsReduced X := by
74	  let pt (Z : irreducibleComponents Y) := Y.presheaf.stalk Z.property.1.genericPoint
75	  have hpt (Z : _) : IsField (pt Z) :=
76	    isField_stalk_of_closure_mem_irreducibleComponents _ _ (by
77	      rw [Z.property.1.closure_genericPoint (isClosed_of_mem_irreducibleComponents _ Z.property)]
78	      exact Z.property)
79	  let (Z : _) := (hpt Z).toField
80	  let Z := ∐ fun Z ↦ Spec (pt Z)
81	  let g : Z ⟶ Y := Sigma.desc fun Z ↦ Y.fromSpecStalk _
82	  have : Finite Z := (sigmaMk _).finite_iff.mp inferInstance
83	  have : QuasiCompact g := ⟨fun _ _ _ ↦ (Set.toFinite _).isCompact⟩
84	  have H : IsSchemeTheoreticallyDominant g := by
85	    rw [isSchemeTheoreticallyDominant_iff_isDominant, isDominant_iff, denseRange_iff_closure_range,
86	      Set.eq_univ_iff_forall]
87	    intro y
88	    let z : Z := Sigma.ι (fun Z ↦ Spec (pt Z)) ⟨_, irreducibleComponent_mem_irreducibleComponents y⟩
89	      (IsLocalRing.closedPoint _)
90	    have hz : g z ⤳ y := by
91	      simp only [g, z, Z, ← Scheme.Hom.comp_apply, Sigma.ι_desc, pt,
92	        Scheme.fromSpecStalk_closedPoint]
93	      exact (IsIrreducible.isGenericPoint_genericPoint _
94	        isClosed_irreducibleComponent).specializes mem_irreducibleComponent
95	    exact hz.mem_closed isClosed_closure (subset_closure ⟨_, rfl⟩)
96	  suffices IsReduced (pullback f g) from IsSchemeTheoreticallyDominant.isReduced (pullback.fst f g)
97	  have H := IsUniversalColimit.isPullback_of_isColimit_left
98	    (X := fun Z ↦ Spec (pt Z))
99	    (FinitaryPreExtensive.isUniversal_finiteCoproducts (coproductIsCoproduct _))
100	    (fun Z ↦ Y.fromSpecStalk _) g f _ _ (fun _ ↦ .of_hasPullback _ _) (coproductIsCoproduct _)
101	  apply +allowSynthFailures @isReduced_of_isOpenImmersion (f := H.isoPullback.inv)
102	  apply +allowSynthFailures @IsReduced.of_openCover (𝒰 := sigmaOpenCover _)
103	  exact fun i ↦ GeometricallyReduced.geometrically_isReduced _ _ _ (.of_hasPullback _ _)
104	
105	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
106	lemma GeometricallyReduced.isReduced_of_flat_of_isLocallyNoetherian
107	    (f : X ⟶ Y) [GeometricallyReduced f] [Flat f]
108	    [IsReduced Y] [IsLocallyNoetherian Y] : IsReduced X := by
109	  apply +allowSynthFailures @IsReduced.of_openCover (𝒰 := Y.affineCover.pullback₁ f)
110	  intro i
111	  have : IsReduced (Y.affineCover.X i) := isReduced_of_isOpenImmersion (Y.affineCover.f i)
112	  have : Finite ↑(irreducibleComponents ↥(Y.affineCover.X i)) := by
113	    let : IsNoetherian (Y.affineCover.X i) := {}
114	    exact TopologicalSpace.NoetherianSpace.finite_irreducibleComponents
115	  exact isReduced_of_flat_of_finite_irreducibleComponents (pullback.snd _ _)

119

122	    IsReduced (pullback f g) :=
123	  GeometricallyReduced.isReduced_of_flat_of_isLocallyNoetherian (pullback.snd _ _)
124

126	    IsReduced (pullback f g) :=
