100	variable (K : Type*) [Ring K] [Algebra F K]
101	
102	/-- Typeclass for purely inseparable field extensions: an algebraic extension `E / F` is purely
103	inseparable if and only if the minimal polynomial of every element of `E ∖ F` is not separable.
104	
105	We define this for general (commutative) rings and only assume `F` and `E` are fields
106	if this is needed for a proof. -/
107	class IsPurelyInseparable : Prop where
108	  isIntegral : Algebra.IsIntegral F E
109	  inseparable' (x : E) : IsSeparable F x → x ∈ (algebraMap F E).range
110	
111	attribute [instance] IsPurelyInseparable.isIntegral
112	
113	variable {E} in
114	theorem IsPurelyInseparable.isIntegral' [IsPurelyInseparable F E] (x : E) : IsIntegral F x :=
115	  Algebra.IsIntegral.isIntegral _
116	
117	theorem IsPurelyInseparable.isAlgebraic [Nontrivial F] [IsPurelyInseparable F E] :
118	    Algebra.IsAlgebraic F E := inferInstance
119	
120	variable {E}
121	
122	theorem IsPurelyInseparable.inseparable [IsPurelyInseparable F E] :
123	    ∀ x : E, IsSeparable F x → x ∈ (algebraMap F E).range :=
124	  IsPurelyInseparable.inseparable'
125	
126	variable {F}
127	
128	theorem isPurelyInseparable_iff : IsPurelyInseparable F E ↔ ∀ x : E,
129	    IsIntegral F x ∧ (IsSeparable F x → x ∈ (algebraMap F E).range) :=
130	  ⟨fun h x ↦ ⟨h.isIntegral' _ x, h.inseparable' x⟩, fun h ↦ ⟨⟨fun x ↦ (h x).1⟩, fun x ↦ (h x).2⟩⟩
131	
132	variable {K}
133	
134	/-- Transfer `IsPurelyInseparable` across an `AlgEquiv`. -/
135	theorem AlgEquiv.isPurelyInseparable (e : K ≃ₐ[F] E) [IsPurelyInseparable F K] :
136	    IsPurelyInseparable F E := by
137	  refine ⟨⟨fun _ ↦ by rw [← isIntegral_algEquiv e.symm]; exact IsPurelyInseparable.isIntegral' F _⟩,
138	    fun x h ↦ ?_⟩
139	  rw [IsSeparable, ← minpoly.algEquiv_eq e.symm] at h
140	  simpa only [RingHom.mem_range, algebraMap_eq_apply] using IsPurelyInseparable.inseparable F _ h
141	
142	theorem AlgEquiv.isPurelyInseparable_iff (e : K ≃ₐ[F] E) :
143	    IsPurelyInseparable F K ↔ IsPurelyInseparable F E :=
144	  ⟨fun _ ↦ e.isPurelyInseparable, fun _ ↦ e.symm.isPurelyInseparable⟩
145	
146	/-- If `E / F` is an algebraic extension, `F` is separably closed,
147	then `E / F` is purely inseparable. -/
148	instance Algebra.IsAlgebraic.isPurelyInseparable_of_isSepClosed
149	    {F : Type u} {E : Type v} [Field F] [Ring E] [IsDomain E] [Algebra F E]
150	    [Algebra.IsAlgebraic F E] [IsSepClosed F] : IsPurelyInseparable F E :=
151	  ⟨inferInstance, fun x h ↦ minpoly.mem_range_of_degree_eq_one F x <|
152	    IsSepClosed.degree_eq_one_of_irreducible F (minpoly.irreducible
153	      (Algebra.IsIntegral.isIntegral _)) h⟩
154	
155	variable (F E K)
156	
157	/-- If `E / F` is both purely inseparable and separable, then `algebraMap F E` is surjective. -/
158	theorem IsPurelyInseparable.surjective_algebraMap_of_isSeparable
159	    [IsPurelyInseparable F E] [Algebra.IsSeparable F E] : Function.Surjective (algebraMap F E) :=
160	  fun x ↦ IsPurelyInseparable.inseparable F x (Algebra.IsSeparable.isSeparable F x)
161	
162	/-- If `E / F` is both purely inseparable and separable, then `algebraMap F E` is bijective. -/
163	theorem IsPurelyInseparable.bijective_algebraMap_of_isSeparable
164	    [Nontrivial E] [IsDomain F] [IsTorsionFree F E]
165	    [IsPurelyInseparable F E] [Algebra.IsSeparable F E] : Function.Bijective (algebraMap F E) :=
166	  ⟨FaithfulSMul.algebraMap_injective F E, surjective_algebraMap_of_isSeparable F E⟩
167	
168	variable {F E} in
169	/-- If a subalgebra of `E / F` is both purely inseparable and separable, then it is equal
170	to `F`. -/
171	theorem Subalgebra.eq_bot_of_isPurelyInseparable_of_isSeparable (L : Subalgebra F E)
172	    [IsPurelyInseparable F L] [Algebra.IsSeparable F L] : L = ⊥ := bot_unique fun x hx ↦ by
173	  obtain ⟨y, hy⟩ := IsPurelyInseparable.surjective_algebraMap_of_isSeparable F L ⟨x, hx⟩
174	  exact ⟨y, congr_arg (Subalgebra.val _) hy⟩
175	
176	/-- If an intermediate field of `E / F` is both purely inseparable and separable, then it is equal
177	to `F`. -/
178	theorem IntermediateField.eq_bot_of_isPurelyInseparable_of_isSeparable
179	    {F : Type u} {E : Type v} [Field F] [Field E] [Algebra F E] (L : IntermediateField F E)
180	    [IsPurelyInseparable F L] [Algebra.IsSeparable F L] : L = ⊥ := bot_unique fun x hx ↦ by
181	  obtain ⟨y, hy⟩ := IsPurelyInseparable.surjective_algebraMap_of_isSeparable F L ⟨x, hx⟩
182	  exact ⟨y, congr_arg (algebraMap L E) hy⟩
183	
184	/-- If `E / F` is purely inseparable, then the separable closure of `F` in `E` is
185	equal to `F`. -/
186	theorem separableClosure.eq_bot_of_isPurelyInseparable
187	    (F : Type u) (E : Type v) [Field F] [Field E] [Algebra F E] [IsPurelyInseparable F E] :
188	    separableClosure F E = ⊥ :=
189	  bot_unique fun x h ↦ IsPurelyInseparable.inseparable F x (mem_separableClosure_iff.1 h)
190	
191	/-- If `E / F` is an algebraic extension, then the separable closure of `F` in `E` is
192	equal to `F` if and only if `E / F` is purely inseparable. -/
193	theorem separableClosure.eq_bot_iff
194	    {F : Type u} {E : Type v} [Field F] [Field E] [Algebra F E] [Algebra.IsAlgebraic F E] :
195	    separableClosure F E = ⊥ ↔ IsPurelyInseparable F E :=
196	  ⟨fun h ↦ isPurelyInseparable_iff.2 fun x ↦ ⟨Algebra.IsIntegral.isIntegral x, fun hs ↦ by
197	    simpa only [h] using mem_separableClosure_iff.2 hs⟩, fun _ ↦ eq_bot_of_isPurelyInseparable F E⟩
198	
199	instance isPurelyInseparable_self : IsPurelyInseparable F F :=
200	  ⟨inferInstance, fun x _ ↦ ⟨x, rfl⟩⟩
201	
202	section
203	
204	variable (F : Type u) {E : Type v} [Field F] [Ring E] [IsDomain E] [Algebra F E]
205	variable (q : ℕ) [ExpChar F q] (x : E)
206	
207	/-- A field extension `E / F` of exponential characteristic `q` is purely inseparable
208	if and only if for every element `x` of `E`, there exists a natural number `n` such that
209	`x ^ (q ^ n)` is contained in `F`. -/
210	@[stacks 09HE]
211	theorem isPurelyInseparable_iff_pow_mem :
212	    IsPurelyInseparable F E ↔ ∀ x : E, ∃ n : ℕ, x ^ q ^ n ∈ (algebraMap F E).range := by
213	  rw [isPurelyInseparable_iff]
214	  refine ⟨fun h x ↦ ?_, fun h x ↦ ?_⟩
215	  · obtain ⟨g, h1, n, h2⟩ := (minpoly.irreducible (h x).1).hasSeparableContraction q
216	    exact ⟨n, (h _).2 <| h1.of_dvd <| minpoly.dvd F _ <| by
217	      simpa only [expand_aeval, minpoly.aeval] using congr_arg (aeval x) h2⟩
218	  have hdeg := (minpoly.natSepDegree_eq_one_iff_pow_mem q).2 (h x)
219	  have halg : IsIntegral F x := by_contra fun h' ↦ by
220	    simp only [minpoly.eq_zero h', natSepDegree_zero, zero_ne_one] at hdeg
221	  refine ⟨halg, fun hsep ↦ ?_⟩
222	  rwa [hsep.natSepDegree_eq_natDegree, minpoly.natDegree_eq_one_iff] at hdeg
223	
224	theorem IsPurelyInseparable.pow_mem [IsPurelyInseparable F E] :
225	    ∃ n : ℕ, x ^ q ^ n ∈ (algebraMap F E).range :=
226	  (isPurelyInseparable_iff_pow_mem F q).1 ‹_› x
227	
228	end
229	
230	end General
231	
232	variable (F : Type u) (E : Type v) [Field F] [Field E] [Algebra F E]
233	variable (K : Type w) [Field K] [Algebra F K]
234	
235	section Field
236	
237	/-- If `K / E / F` is a field extension tower such that `K / F` is purely inseparable,
238	then `E / F` is also purely inseparable. -/
239	theorem IsPurelyInseparable.tower_bot [Algebra E K] [IsScalarTower F E K]
240	    [IsPurelyInseparable F K] : IsPurelyInseparable F E := by
241	  refine ⟨⟨fun x ↦ (isIntegral' F (algebraMap E K x)).tower_bot_of_field⟩, fun x h ↦ ?_⟩
242	  rw [IsSeparable, ← minpoly.algebraMap_eq (algebraMap E K).injective] at h
243	  obtain ⟨y, h⟩ := inseparable F _ h
244	  exact ⟨y, (algebraMap E K).injective (h.symm ▸ (IsScalarTower.algebraMap_apply F E K y).symm)⟩
245	
246	/-- If `K / E / F` is a field extension tower such that `K / F` is purely inseparable,
247	then `K / E` is also purely inseparable. -/
248	theorem IsPurelyInseparable.tower_top [Algebra E K] [IsScalarTower F E K]
249	    [h : IsPurelyInseparable F K] : IsPurelyInseparable E K := by
250	  obtain ⟨q, _⟩ := ExpChar.exists F
251	  haveI := expChar_of_injective_algebraMap (algebraMap F E).injective q
252	  rw [isPurelyInseparable_iff_pow_mem _ q] at h ⊢
253	  intro x
254	  obtain ⟨n, y, h⟩ := h x
255	  exact ⟨n, (algebraMap F E) y, h.symm ▸ (IsScalarTower.algebraMap_apply F E K y).symm⟩
256	
257	/-- If `E / F` and `K / E` are both purely inseparable extensions, then `K / F` is also
258	purely inseparable. -/
259	@[stacks 02JJ "See also 00GM"]
260	theorem IsPurelyInseparable.trans [Algebra E K] [IsScalarTower F E K]
261	    [h1 : IsPurelyInseparable F E] [h2 : IsPurelyInseparable E K] : IsPurelyInseparable F K := by
262	  obtain ⟨q, _⟩ := ExpChar.exists F
263	  haveI := expChar_of_injective_algebraMap (algebraMap F E).injective q
264	  rw [isPurelyInseparable_iff_pow_mem _ q] at h1 h2 ⊢
265	  intro x
266	  obtain ⟨n, y, h2⟩ := h2 x
267	  obtain ⟨m, z, h1⟩ := h1 y
268	  refine ⟨n + m, z, ?_⟩
269	  rw [IsScalarTower.algebraMap_apply F E K, h1, map_pow, h2, ← pow_mul, ← pow_add]
270	
271	namespace IntermediateField
272	
273	variable (M : IntermediateField F K)
274	
275	instance isPurelyInseparable_tower_bot [IsPurelyInseparable F K] : IsPurelyInseparable F M :=
276	  IsPurelyInseparable.tower_bot F M K
277	
278	instance isPurelyInseparable_tower_top [IsPurelyInseparable F K] : IsPurelyInseparable M K :=
279	  IsPurelyInseparable.tower_top F M K
280	
281	end IntermediateField
282	
283	variable {E}
284	
285	/-- A field extension `E / F` is purely inseparable if and only if for every element `x` of `E`,
286	its minimal polynomial has separable degree one. -/
287	theorem isPurelyInseparable_iff_natSepDegree_eq_one :
288	    IsPurelyInseparable F E ↔ ∀ x : E, (minpoly F x).natSepDegree = 1 := by
289	  obtain ⟨q, _⟩ := ExpChar.exists F
290	  simp_rw [isPurelyInseparable_iff_pow_mem F q, minpoly.natSepDegree_eq_one_iff_pow_mem q]
291	
292	theorem IsPurelyInseparable.natSepDegree_eq_one [IsPurelyInseparable F E] (x : E) :
293	    (minpoly F x).natSepDegree = 1 :=
294	  (isPurelyInseparable_iff_natSepDegree_eq_one F).1 ‹_› x
295	
296	/-- A field extension `E / F` of exponential characteristic `q` is purely inseparable
297	if and only if for every element `x` of `E`, the minimal polynomial of `x` over `F` is of form
298	`X ^ (q ^ n) - y` for some natural number `n` and some element `y` of `F`. -/
299	theorem isPurelyInseparable_iff_minpoly_eq_X_pow_sub_C (q : ℕ) [hF : ExpChar F q] :
300	    IsPurelyInseparable F E ↔ ∀ x : E, ∃ (n : ℕ) (y : F), minpoly F x = X ^ q ^ n - C y := by
301	  simp_rw [isPurelyInseparable_iff_natSepDegree_eq_one,
302	    minpoly.natSepDegree_eq_one_iff_eq_X_pow_sub_C q]
303	
304	theorem IsPurelyInseparable.minpoly_eq_X_pow_sub_C (q : ℕ) [ExpChar F q] [IsPurelyInseparable F E]
305	    (x : E) : ∃ (n : ℕ) (y : F), minpoly F x = X ^ q ^ n - C y :=
306	  (isPurelyInseparable_iff_minpoly_eq_X_pow_sub_C F q).1 ‹_› x
307	
308	/-- A field extension `E / F` of exponential characteristic `q` is purely inseparable
309	if and only if for every element `x` of `E`, the minimal polynomial of `x` over `F` is of form
310	`(X - x) ^ (q ^ n)` for some natural number `n`. -/
311	theorem isPurelyInseparable_iff_minpoly_eq_X_sub_C_pow (q : ℕ) [hF : ExpChar F q] :
312	    IsPurelyInseparable F E ↔
313	    ∀ x : E, ∃ n : ℕ, (minpoly F x).map (algebraMap F E) = (X - C x) ^ q ^ n := by
314	  simp_rw [isPurelyInseparable_iff_natSepDegree_eq_one,
315	    minpoly.natSepDegree_eq_one_iff_eq_X_sub_C_pow q]
316	
317	theorem IsPurelyInseparable.minpoly_eq_X_sub_C_pow (q : ℕ) [ExpChar F q] [IsPurelyInseparable F E]
318	    (x : E) : ∃ n : ℕ, (minpoly F x).map (algebraMap F E) = (X - C x) ^ q ^ n :=
319	  (isPurelyInseparable_iff_minpoly_eq_X_sub_C_pow F q).1 ‹_› x
320	
321	variable (E) in
322	lemma IsPurelyInseparable.finrank_eq_pow
323	    (q : ℕ) [ExpChar F q] [IsPurelyInseparable F E] [FiniteDimensional F E] :
324	    ∃ n, finrank F E = q ^ n := by
325	  suffices ∀ (F E : Type v) [Field F] [Field E] [Algebra F E] (q : ℕ) [ExpChar F q]
326	      [IsPurelyInseparable F E] [FiniteDimensional F E], ∃ n, finrank F E = q ^ n by
327	    simpa using this (⊥ : IntermediateField F E) E q
328	  intro F E _ _ _ q _ _ _
329	  generalize hd : finrank F E = d
330	  induction d using Nat.strongRecOn generalizing F with
331	  | ind d IH =>
332	    by_cases h : (⊥ : IntermediateField F E) = ⊤
333	    · rw [← finrank_top', ← h, IntermediateField.finrank_bot] at hd
334	      exact ⟨0, ((pow_zero q).trans hd).symm⟩
335	    obtain ⟨x, -, hx⟩ := SetLike.exists_of_lt (lt_of_le_of_ne bot_le h :)
336	    obtain ⟨m, y, e⟩ := IsPurelyInseparable.minpoly_eq_X_pow_sub_C F q x
337	    have : finrank F F⟮x⟯ = q ^ m := by
338	      rw [adjoin.finrank (Algebra.IsIntegral.isIntegral x), e, natDegree_sub_C, natDegree_X_pow]
339	    obtain ⟨n, hn⟩ := IH _ (by
340	      rw [← hd, ← finrank_mul_finrank F F⟮x⟯, Nat.lt_mul_iff_one_lt_left finrank_pos, this]
341	      by_contra! H
342	      refine hx (finrank_adjoin_simple_eq_one_iff.mp (le_antisymm (this ▸ H) ?_))
343	      exact Nat.one_le_iff_ne_zero.mpr Module.finrank_pos.ne') (F⟮x⟯) rfl
344	    exact ⟨m + n, by rw [← hd, ← finrank_mul_finrank F F⟮x⟯, hn, pow_add, this]⟩
345

17	/-!
18	
19	# Smooth morphisms
20	
21	In this file we define smooth morphisms. The main definitions are:
22	
23	- `AlgebraicGeometry.Smooth`: A morphism of schemes `f : X ⟶ Y` is smooth if for each affine `U ⊆ Y`
24	  and `V ⊆ f ⁻¹' U`, the induced map `Γ(Y, U) ⟶ Γ(X, V)` is smooth.
25

28	  and an affine open neighborhood `U` of `f.base x` with `V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U` such that the induced
29	  map `Γ(Y, U) ⟶ Γ(X, V)` is standard smooth (of relative dimension `n`).
30

34	  if and only if for each `x : X` there exists an affine open neighborhood `V` of `x`

540	An element `x` of an algebra `K` over a commutative ring `F` is said to be *separable*, if its
541	minimal polynomial over `K` is separable. Note that the minimal polynomial of any element not
542	integral over `F` is defined to be `0`, which is not a separable polynomial.
543	-/
544	@[stacks 09H1 "second part"]
545	def IsSeparable (x : K) : Prop := Polynomial.Separable (minpoly F x)
546	
547	/-- Typeclass for separable field extension: `K` is a separable field extension of `F` iff
548	the minimal polynomial of every `x : K` is separable. This implies that `K/F` is an algebraic
549	extension, because the minimal polynomial of a non-integral element is `0`, which is not
550	separable.
551	
552	We define this for general (commutative) rings and only assume `F` and `K` are fields if this
553	is needed for a proof. -/
554	@[mk_iff isSeparable_def, stacks 09H1 "third part"]
555	protected class Algebra.IsSeparable : Prop where
556	  isSeparable' : ∀ x : K, IsSeparable F x
557	
558	variable {K}
559	
560	theorem Algebra.IsSeparable.isSeparable [Algebra.IsSeparable F K] : ∀ x : K, IsSeparable F x :=
561	  Algebra.IsSeparable.isSeparable'
562	
563	variable {F} in
564	/-- If the minimal polynomial of `x : K` over `F` is separable, then `x` is integral over `F`,
565	because the minimal polynomial of a non-integral element is `0`, which is not separable. -/
566	theorem IsSeparable.isIntegral {x : K} (h : IsSeparable F x) : IsIntegral F x := by
567	  cases subsingleton_or_nontrivial F
568	  · haveI := Module.subsingleton F K
569	    exact ⟨1, monic_one, Subsingleton.elim _ _⟩
570	  · exact of_not_not (h.ne_zero <| minpoly.eq_zero ·)
571	
572	theorem Algebra.IsSeparable.isIntegral [Algebra.IsSeparable F K] : ∀ x : K, IsIntegral F x :=
573	  fun x ↦ _root_.IsSeparable.isIntegral (Algebra.IsSeparable.isSeparable F x)
574	
575	variable (K) in
576	instance Algebra.IsSeparable.isAlgebraic [Nontrivial F] [Algebra.IsSeparable F K] :
577	    Algebra.IsAlgebraic F K :=
578	  ⟨fun x ↦ (Algebra.IsSeparable.isIntegral F x).isAlgebraic⟩
579	
580	variable {F}

584	  ⟨fun _ x => ⟨Algebra.IsSeparable.isIntegral F x, Algebra.IsSeparable.isSeparable F x⟩,

namespace AlgebraicGeometry

/-- The canonical `k → Γ(X, O_X)` ring map for `X` over `Spec k`: precompose

def gammaAlgebraMap (k : Type u) [CommRing k] (X : Scheme.{u})
    [X.Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))] :
    (CommRingCat.of k) ⟶ X.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ⊤) :=
  (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)).inv ≫ (X ↘ Spec (CommRingCat.of k)).appTop

/-- The canonical `Algebra k Γ(X, O_X)` structure on global sections of `X` over
`Spec k`. Matches the local `algkΓ` definition used by the consumer in
`AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/ChartAlgebra.lean` (`constants_integral_over_base_field`). -/
noncomputable def gammaAlgebra (k : Type u) [CommRing k] (X : Scheme.{u})
    [X.Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))] :
    Algebra k ↥(X.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ⊤)) :=
  (gammaAlgebraMap k X).hom.toAlgebra

    [X.Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))] :
    (CommRingCat.of k) ⟶ X.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ⊤) :=

145:def finSepDegree : ℕ := Nat.card (Emb F E)

132	variable (F : Type u) (E : Type v) [Field F] [Field E] [Algebra F E]

134	
135	namespace Field
136	
137	/-- `Field.Emb F E` is the type of `F`-algebra homomorphisms from `E` to the algebraic closure
138	of `E`. -/
139	abbrev Emb := E →ₐ[F] AlgebraicClosure E
140	
141	/-- If `E / F` is an algebraic extension, then the (finite) separable degree of `E / F`
142	is the number of `F`-algebra homomorphisms from `E` to the algebraic closure of `E`,
143	as a natural number. It is defined to be zero if there are infinitely many of them.
144	Note that if `E / F` is not algebraic, then this definition makes no mathematical sense. -/
145	def finSepDegree : ℕ := Nat.card (Emb F E)
146	
147	instance instInhabitedEmb : Inhabited (Emb F E) := ⟨IsScalarTower.toAlgHom F E _⟩
148	
149	instance instNeZeroFinSepDegree [FiniteDimensional F E] : NeZero (finSepDegree F E) :=
150	  ⟨Nat.card_ne_zero.2 ⟨inferInstance, Fintype.finite <| minpoly.AlgHom.fintype _ _ _⟩⟩
151	
152	/-- A random bijection between `Field.Emb F E` and `Field.Emb F K` when `E` and `K` are isomorphic
153	as `F`-algebras. -/
154	def embEquivOfEquiv (i : E ≃ₐ[F] K) :
155	    Emb F E ≃ Emb F K := AlgEquiv.arrowCongr i <| AlgEquiv.symm <| by
156	  let _ : Algebra E K := i.toAlgHom.toRingHom.toAlgebra
157	  have : Algebra.IsAlgebraic E K := by
158	    constructor
159	    intro x
160	    have h := isAlgebraic_algebraMap (R := E) (A := K) (i.symm.toAlgHom x)
161	    rw [show ∀ y : E, (algebraMap E K) y = i.toAlgHom y from fun y ↦ rfl] at h

\chapter{Chart-algebra (S3) sub-claims (piece (ii) Lane 1 --- Lean file)}
\label{chap:cotangent-chartalgebra-s3}

This chapter is a pointer to the Lean file
\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/ChartAlgebraS3.lean}. The mathematical
content itself lives in chapter~\ref{chap:RigidityKbar}
(\texttt{RigidityKbar.tex} \S "Chart-algebra piece (ii) first-class
decomposition") and is not duplicated here. This chapter exists to satisfy
the per-Lean-file blueprint convention (one chapter per Lean file).

\textbf{File scope (iter-149 Lane 1 scaffolding).} The file scaffolds the
four (S3.*) sub-claims promoted to first-class blueprint declarations by
the iter-149 \texttt{blueprint-writer-rigiditykbar-iter149} writer round:

\begin{itemize}
  \item \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.isGeometricallyReduced\_Gamma\_of\_smooth}
    --- \cref{lem:S3_sep_1_smooth_geometrically_reduced_Gamma} (S3.sep.1).
  \item \texttt{Algebra.IsSeparable.of\_isGeometricallyReduced\_of\_finite}
    --- \cref{lem:S3_sep_2_geom_reduced_finite_field_ext_is_separable} (S3.sep.2).
  \item \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Gamma\_baseChange\_iso\_tensor\_of\_proper}
    --- \cref{lem:S3_pi_1_Gamma_baseChange_iso_tensor_of_proper} (S3.pi.1).
  \item \texttt{Algebra.IsPurelyInseparable.of\_unique\_minPrime\_baseChange}
    --- \cref{lem:S3_pi_2_isPurelyInseparable_of_unique_minPrime_baseChange} (S3.pi.2).
\end{itemize}

The file also exports a shared algebra-instance helper
\texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.gammaAlgebra} (induced from the structure
morphism \(X \to \Spec k\)) used uniformly across the four sub-claims and
their Lane 2 consumer in
\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/ChartAlgebra.lean}
(\texttt{constants\_integral\_over\_base\_field}).

\textbf{Body status (iter-149 prover lane).} All four declarations are
present with their correct first-class signatures; bodies are structured
\texttt{sorry}s with documented closure paths inline. Iter-149 PARTIAL was
explicitly authorised on (S3.pi.1) by the plan agent. Iter-150+ closure
strategy:

\begin{itemize}
  \item (S3.sep.1) consumes (S3.pi.1) via the standalone Mathlib bridge
    \texttt{Smooth \(\Rightarrow\) GeometricallyReduced (scheme morphism)};
    Stacks Tags \textbf{0334} + \textbf{04QM}.
  \item (S3.sep.2) consumes the Artinian-product chase: \(F \otimes_k \bar k\)
    finite-dim reduced \(\Rightarrow\) product of fields \(\Rightarrow\)
    each factor \(= \bar k\) \(\Rightarrow\) embedding count = degree
    \(\Rightarrow\) separable. Stacks Tag \textbf{0BJF}.
  \item (S3.pi.1) is the \v Cech-equaliser + flat-tensor exchange chase of
    Stacks Tag \textbf{02KH} specialised to \(H^0 = \Gamma\).
  \item (S3.pi.2) consumes the local-Artinian-reduction argument:
    unique minimal prime in finite-dim \(\bar k\)-algebra \(\Rightarrow\)
    local Artinian \(\Rightarrow\) (Stacks Tag \textbf{05DH}) purely
    inseparable.
\end{itemize}

The full mathematical content (including the \emph{Literature.} blocks and
the cross-references to \cref{lem:constants_integral_over_base_field}) is
in \texttt{blueprint/src/chapters/RigidityKbar.tex} \S "Chart-algebra
piece (ii) first-class decomposition".
